data.title: Cathie Wood Buys the Dip in Joby Aviation (JOBY), Sells Teradyne (TER) Near Highs
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made notable portfolio moves on Thursday, January 29, as revealed in daily fund disclosures. These trades underscore ARK's continued optimism on disruptive technologies like electric vehicles (EVs), biotech, fintech, autonomous driving, and semiconductors.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The largest trade was the purchase of 781,519 shares of flying-taxi maker Joby Aviation (JOBY) for about $10.45 million. ARK bought these shares through the ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ) and the ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX).

JOBY stock crashed 16.7% yesterday after news of a fresh capital raise, as investors worried about further equity dilution. The air taxi company aims to raise about $1 billion through a sale of common stock and convertible senior notes due 2032.

Wood also added 127,184 shares of gene-editing company CRISPR Therapeutics AG (CRSP) for roughly $6.83 million. This trade highlights Wood's continued interest in the biotech sector. CRSP stock dipped marginally amid a broader market decline.

Wood Capitalizes on Teradyne's 52-Week High

On the sell-side, Wood dumped 31,668 shares of Teradyne (TER) for $7.93 million, capitalizing on the stock's intraday high of $253.61. Teradyne and MultiLane announced a joint venture called MultiLane Test Products (MLTP) to meet rising demand for high-speed data connection tests in AI data centers.

The deal is expected to close in the first half of 2026, pending approvals. Under the deal, MultiLane will contribute its test/measurement assets to the JV, while retaining interconnects and data center test businesses. Teradyne will hold the majority ownership.

ARK also sold 31,463 shares of Veracyte (VCYT) for $1.21 million and cut its Illumina (ILMN) stake by 35,897 shares, continuing its recent divestments in the stock.

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool:

Currently, analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating on Teradyne and CRISPR, with CRSP stock offering the highest upside potential between them. Meanwhile, analysts have a "Hold" on Joby Aviation, despite expecting a 41% upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Down after S&P 500 Posts Second Straight Loss, Microsoft Stock Slides 10%
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down late Thursday night after the market posted a second straight losing session. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.71%, 0.5%, and 0.57%, respectively, at 1:15 p.m. EDT on January 30.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular session, the S&P 500 ended 0.1% lower, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite fell 0.7%. The Dow rose 0.11%, or nearly 56 points. A sharp post-earnings drop in Microsoft (MSFT) weighed on both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq, with the stock sliding about 10% for its worst one-day decline since March 2020.

Late Thursday, President Donald Trump said he plans to announce his choice to replace Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on Friday morning, adding another point of uncertainty for markets.

In after-hours trading, Apple (AAPL) shares edged higher after the company reported fiscal first-quarter earnings and revenue that topped expectations, helped by a sharp jump in iPhone sales.

Looking ahead, investors are waiting for SoFi Technologies (SOFI) to report its fourth-quarter earnings on Friday.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: China Greenlights DeepSeek Nvidia H200 Chip Deal — with Conditions
data.description:
China has reportedly granted its leading AI startup, DeepSeek, approval to purchase Nvidia's (NVDA) H200 AI chips. Reuters, citing sources familiar with the matter, reported that the regulatory conditions are still being finalized. Despite the approval, NVDA is down in pre-market trading as investors wait to see the exact terms and how the deal will benefit Nvidia.

data.title: "Massive Expansion Ahead" as Starbucks (SBUX) Targets over 2,000 New Stores Globally
data.description:
Starbucks (SBUX) has outlined plans to open thousands of new stores worldwide as it works to boost momentum and strengthen its global footprint. CFO Cathy Smith disclosed that the company aims to add more than 2,000 stores globally in Fiscal 2028, which is more than three times the number it expects to add this year. The announcement was made at a recent investor event following the company's mixed fiscal first-quarter results.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In the U.S., the company plans to build 400 new company‑operated stores in 2028, with the potential to add as many as 5,000 more in the years that follow. Most of that growth will target the central, southern, and northeastern regions, areas where Starbucks is facing rising competition from fast‑growing drive‑through chains like 7 Brew and Dutch Bros. (BROS).

The move marks a return to expansion after the company closed several underperforming locations last year, signaling confidence in its long-term strategy.

New Store Formats and Lower Costs

Future Starbucks locations will work as all-in-one hubs, supporting drive-through, mobile orders, delivery, and traditional in-store service. In busy city areas where drive-throughs do not fit, the company will use smaller cafe-style formats instead.

One of the newest concepts is the "Ristretto" store, a smaller design named after the espresso shot. Though smaller than a typical Starbucks, it still includes seating and a full coffee bar. The company expects updated designs to reduce building costs by roughly 20%.

Big International Ambitions

Starbucks currently operates nearly 23,000 stores outside the U.S., and executives believe that number could climb to around 40,000 over time. The company sees opportunity in China, where it plans to add 15,000 to 20,000 new locations on top of the 8,000 it already runs.

The company recently agreed to sell a majority stake in its China business to Boyu Capital, a move that will shift most international stores to a licensed model. Once the deal closes, about 90% of Starbucks' overseas locations will be run by licensees, up from 55% currently.

Overall, the company is gearing up for a new phase of growth with thousands of new stores on the way and a stronger focus on employees and customers.

Is Starbucks Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Starbucks stock based on 13 Buy, seven Hold, and two Sell recommendations. The average SBUX stock price target of $98.22 implies an upside potential of 1.96%.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: SanDisk Stock Explodes 17% on Q2 Earnings Beat — Buy SNDK or Wait?
data.description:
SanDisk (SNDK) stock jumped sharply after the company delivered a standout Q2 earnings report and guidance, blowing past Wall Street estimates on both profit and revenue. Having said that, the stock's solid rally raises the risk of near-term volatility as some investors lock in profits. For long-term investors who believe AI-driven storage demand is still in the early innings, any pullbacks could present attractive entry points. Overall, SNDK remains compelling for patient investors—but chasing the stock immediately after a big spike may require caution.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SNDK stock jumped 17.17% in pre-market trading on Friday. Year-to-date, the shares are up more than 125%.

AI Boom Powers SanDisk Profit and Revenue Surge

SanDisk's fiscal second-quarter profit jumped 672% year-over-year to $803 million, or $5.15 per share. Meanwhile, revenue climbed 61% to $3.03 billion, beating analysts' $2.69 billion estimate.

The company's data-center revenue jumped 76% from a year earlier, as artificial intelligence continued to drive a sharp increase in demand.

What Lies Ahead for SanDisk?

Looking ahead, the company said it expects revenue of $4.4 billion to $4.8 billion for the next quarter, far above Wall Street's $2.9 billion estimate. Even the midpoint of the range implies nearly 60% upside versus expectations. SanDisk also guided for adjusted EPS of $12 to $14, compared with analysts' forecasts of just $5.11—a gap of more than 150%.

SanDisk's outlook underscores just how quickly AI-driven demand is accelerating, suggesting that earnings momentum is still building rather than peaking. Despite the strong quarter, management said the company was unable to fully meet surging demand and expects this supply-demand imbalance to persist into 2026. That level of visibility supports continued revenue growth and sustained pricing power.

At the same time, SanDisk's sharp improvement in profitability signals that operating leverage is kicking in as volumes rise.

What Is the Price Target for SanDisk?

Wall Street analysts currently rate SanDisk stock a Moderate Buy, with 10 Buy ratings and four Holds issued over the past three months. After the recent rally, the shares are now trading above SanDisk's average price target of $395.93, implying a downside of 26.6% from current levels.

That said, these ratings and targets are likely to change as analysts update their forecasts following the latest earnings report.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Robinhood Stock (HOOD) Pops on 'Trump Accounts' Trustee Role News
data.description:
Shares of Robinhood Markets (HOOD) rallied as much as 4% in after-hours trading on January 29. The surge followed news that the U.S. is considering giving the fintech platform a key role in overseeing the new "Trump Accounts" being created for millions of children. According to Bloomberg, a selection is expected soon, with the Treasury Department eyeing up to three companies as initial trustees, including banks and nonbanks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, Robinhood has already started internal preparations in case it is selected as a trustee, according to people familiar with the matter. Meanwhile, some major brokerages like Fidelity Investments and Vanguard Group are not on the initial candidate list.

What Are Trump Accounts?

President Donald Trump introduced Trump Accounts as part of his One Big Beautiful Bill Act. The program seeds $1,000 individual retirement accounts for children born from 2025 through 2028. These tax-advantaged savings accounts are aimed at promoting long-term savings for goals like retirement, education, or homeownership.

These accounts will be invested in U.S. stock index funds and locked until the child turns 18. The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) said that the funds should have no annual fees and expenses of no more than 0.1%.

How Will Robinhood Benefit?

As a prime trustee, Robinhood could earn billions of dollars in new assets to manage. Although customers will have the option to transfer funds to new providers, initial trustees could gain early access to fund launches, potentially managing billions in new assets and attracting customers for additional products. However, onboarding a broad range of clients, many of whom may be unfamiliar with similar investment funds, presents a significant operational challenge, requiring robust customer support systems.

Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev previously said the company has been "very engaged" in the Trump Accounts process since the start and also attended a White House event focused on the initiative. In December, he told the President that Robinhood could provide the technology and resources to make the accounts "as robust and intuitive as possible." He had also stated, "I think this is super important, particularly now, because getting people from a young age to buy into our free-market system and be owners of America. I think that could solve a lot of the problem."

Importance of Secondary Players

Notably, some of America's biggest banks like JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), and Bank of America (BAC) have expressed interest in secondary roles for Trump Account rollovers rather than serving as initial trustees. They see it as a simpler and less costly way to be involved and gain access to millions of new customers. Spokespersons for Fidelity and Vanguard said their firms are working with the administration to ensure customers can access rollover accounts beyond the initial providers.

Bank of America said that it is continuing to engage with the administration on the best way to make Trump Account rollovers accessible to its clients. It is also going to offer Trump Accounts, with matching and pretax contributions, to eligible employees.

Is HOOD a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, Robinhood stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 15 Buys and four Hold ratings. The average Robinhood price target of $154.94 implies 53% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, HOOD stock has surged over 91%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Lose Steam on Tech Weakness as Trump Prepares Fed Chair Pick
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Thursday as tech and software stocks sold off sharply.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Microsoft (MSFT) closed with a 9.9% loss despite reporting higher-than-expected revenue and adjusted earnings per share (EPS) for the period ended December 31. Microsoft joined a broad selloff in software stocks, with ServiceNow (NOW), Salesforce (CRM), and Workday (WDAY) also posting steep drawdowns. Investors remain concerned about the disruptive effects of AI that could potentially intensify competition in the software space and lower margins.

During a Cabinet meeting, President Trump said that he would announce his nominee for the Fed Chair position "sometime next week." Trump ramped up criticism of Fed Chair Jerome Powell after the central bank voted 10-2 on Wednesday to hold rates steady between 3.50% and 3.75%. Powell's term is set to end on May 15, although his term as a Governor will last until January 31, 2028.

"He is hurting our Country, and its National Security," Trump said in a Truth Social post on Thursday. "We should have a substantially lower rate now that even this moron admits inflation is no longer a problem or threat."

BlackRock's (BLK) Rick Rieder is the front-runner to be nominated by Trump to lead the Fed, according to prediction platform Polymarket. Rieder holds 38% odds, followed by former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh at 30% and current Fed Governor Christopher Waller at 14%.

Before the market open, the Department of Labor (DOL) announced that initial jobless claims fell by 1,000 to 209,000, above the consensus estimate of 205,000. In addition, the DOL revised the prior week's claims to 210,000 from 200,000. Initial jobless claims are an indicator of layoffs and track how many people have applied for unemployment benefits for the first time. The data comes after Amazon (AMZN) announced it would lay off 16,000 employees, bringing its total since October to 30,000.

Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by one week and track the number of people continuing to receive unemployment benefits, fell by 38,000 to 1.827 million and were below the estimate of 1.850 million. However, the DOL revised the prior week's figure to 1.865 million from 1.849 million.

Elsewhere, the U.S. trade deficit for November nearly doubled to $56.8 billion from $29.2 billion in October, driven by a 5% rise in imports to $348.9 billion. Rising imports are a headwind to gross domestic product (GDP), as they reduce net exports.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.13% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.53%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "Reward for Progress": Wells Fargo (WFC) Lifts CEO Charlie Scharf's Pay by 28%
data.description:
Wells Fargo (WFC) has awarded CEO Charlie Scharf a significant pay increase for 2025. His total compensation of $40 million reflects a 28% jump from the prior year, citing meaningful progress under Scharf's leadership.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Scharf's 2025 pay package includes a $2.5 million base salary, a $9.4 million cash bonus, and the remainder in stock awards.

According to the filing, the board credited Scharf with resolving major compliance issues that have weighed on the bank for years, including closing out several regulatory penalties tied to past scandals. The bank also pointed to rising earnings and revenue as key factors behind the compensation boost.

In Line with Wall Street's Broader Trend

Scharf's pay increase mirrors a broader pattern across major U.S. banks, where top executives saw compensation climb after a strong year for the industry. This comes after a turbulent few years marked by regulatory pressure, higher funding costs, and shifting interest rate expectations.

Boards have rewarded top leaders for navigating those challenges, stabilizing operations, and delivering improved returns for shareholders.

Other bank chiefs have seen similar increases. Goldman Sachs (GS) CEO David Solomon received a 21% raise to $47 million, while JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon saw his compensation rise just over 10% to $43 million.

Is WFC a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, WFC stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys, seven Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $98.93, the average Wells Fargo stock price target implies a 9.15% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
